Alarm clock: My alarm clock didn't go off, and I overslept.
It is pouring outside: Take an umbrella — it is pouring outside.
A blessing in disguise: Losing that job was a blessing in disguise.
Force =make: Nobody forced him to sign the contract.
Run errands: I spent Saturday running errands around town.
Own: It's my own recipe, not from a book.
Cut the shit: Cut the shit and tell me what happened.
Come up with = make up = create: She came up with a brilliant name for the café.
Rush hour: Avoid the metro at rush hour.
Pass away = die: His grandfather passed away last spring.
Take part in =participate in: Our team took part in the city marathon.
Give a go= give a try: Give the new recipe a try.
Bargain: At the market you can always bargain a little.
Warn: They warned us about the traffic.
Act: He acted strangely at the party.
Blame (for): Don't blame me for the weather.
Foresee: Nobody could foresee such a result.
Predict: It's hard to predict prices these days.
Unpredictable: The weather in April is unpredictable.
Notice: Did you notice her new haircut?
Estimate: The builder estimated the work at two weeks.
Expand on: Please expand on that point a little.
Could you expand on it?: Interesting idea — could you expand on it?
Around = give or take: The bag costs around five thousand.
I wonder: I wonder why the shop is closed today.
Peer: Teenagers care a lot about their peers' opinion.
Get on (well) with = find common languag: She gets on well with everyone at work.
Shift: He works the night shift this week.
Greet: The host greeted us at the door.
Back then: Back then we didn't have smartphones.
Believe: I believe you — no need to explain.
As for: As for dessert, let's share one.
Side-hustle (part-time job): Photography started as a side hustle for him.
A change of scenery: I need a change of scenery — maybe a weekend trip.
Immense: The support from friends was immense.
Fail: He failed the driving test twice.
I'm up/down for it: Movie night? I'm down for it.
Remind about/of: Remind me about the meeting tomorrow.
I'm not done yet!: Wait, I'm not done yet — one more point.
Like the back of my hand: I know this district like the back of my hand.
Squad: Our little squad meets every Friday.
Frequent = often: He's a frequent guest at this café.
Cost a fortune = cost an arm and a leg =: That jacket costs an arm and a leg.
In fact: In fact, the museum is free on Sundays.
In the best-case scenario: In the best-case scenario, we finish by Friday.
In the worst-case scenario: In the worst-case scenario, we'll reschedule.
Eat out /eat in: Let's eat out tonight — I'm too tired to cook.
Put up with sth: How do you put up with that noise?
Accept =embrace: It took time to embrace the changes.
Urgent: Sorry, I have an urgent call.
Have to: I have to finish the report today.
Have (a lot/a little/nothing) in common: We have nothing in common, but we're friends.
Mingle = communicate: He hates mingling at big parties.
For some reason: For some reason, the code doesn't work today.
Be packed with = be crowded with: The train was packed with tourists.
Be in good/great/bad shape: After a year of swimming he's in great shape.
Break the ice: A simple joke helps to break the ice.
Handle = deal with = cope with: Let me deal with the documents.
Define: First, let's define the main goal.
Definition: Give me a simple definition of this word.
Definite (ly): I'll definitely come to your birthday.
Overwhelm: The number of options overwhelmed me.
Every cloud has a silver lining: The flight was canceled, but every cloud has a silver lining — we saw the old town.
Save for a rainy day: Put ten percent aside — save for a rainy day.
Blessing in disguise: The delay turned out to be a blessing in disguise.
Drive up the wall: That dripping tap drives me up the wall.
Pick up lines: His pick-up lines are old but funny.
Be fond of +Ving= be keen on +Ving= be i: They're fond of hiking in the mountains.
Dig deeper: The numbers look fine, but let's dig deeper.
Tag questions: You speak English, don't you? — that's a tag question.
A cent: The app doesn't cost a cent.
A dime =10 cents: He found a dime under the sofa.
A quarter: Insert a quarter to use the cart.
A dime a dozen: Coffee shops are a dime a dozen in this area.
Remind of: This song reminds me of our trip.
Remind about: Remind me about the tickets, please.
Boast = brag: He keeps bragging about his new car.
Hurt: Her cold words really hurt me.
Attempt: His first attempt to bake bread failed.
Remain: Only two tickets remain for tonight.
Show up: He showed up an hour late, as usual.
It's your turn: I cooked yesterday — it's your turn today.
Be into sth = be fond of = be keen on: Lately I'm into old black-and-white films.
Be familiar with sth: Are you familiar with this app?
Fall in love with = be head over heels w: He's head over heels with his new neighbor.
Come to the rescue: The battery died, but a stranger came to the rescue.
I doubt it!: Will he come on time? I doubt it!
Depend on: The picnic depends on the weather.
Turn to: When I need advice, I turn to my sister.
Gravitate towards/to: I always gravitate towards the window seat.
Be able to: Soon you'll be able to read books in English.
Suffer (from): He suffers from insomnia before exams.
Immerse into =dive into: Immerse into the language: films, podcasts, books.
Butterflies in the stomach: Before the interview I had butterflies in my stomach.
I mean = want to say: It's fine — I mean, we can do better, but it's fine.
Long story short = in a nutshell: Long story short, we got the flat.
Aim = target = goal =purpose: The aim of the lesson is new vocabulary.
Aim at +Ving: The course aims at improving your speaking.
Breadwinner = bring home the bacon: After dad retired, mom became the breadwinner.
Victim: He was the victim of a phone scammer.
Support: Thanks for your support during the exams.
Keep in mind: Keep in mind: the museum closes at six.
Coincide: My vacation coincides with the holidays.
Free time = spare time = leisure time: In my free time I play badminton.
Curve: Slow down — there's a sharp curve ahead.
Cut in = interrupt: He always cuts in with his own stories.
Cheat on smb: She left him because he cheated on her.
Heartbreaking: The end of the film is heartbreaking.
End up +Ving: We ended up ordering pizza at midnight.
What it is like: Tell me what it is like to live abroad.
At first sight: It was love at first sight.
Confuse (with): I always confuse him with his twin brother.
Involve in: Don't involve me in your quarrel.
Deal with: I'll deal with the emails after lunch.
I'm all ears!: You have news? I'm all ears!
Be going to: We are going to visit my aunt on Sunday.
Impress: Her fluent English impressed the whole office.
Rush to: He rushed to the airport without breakfast.
Improve: My pronunciation improved after a month of practice.
Cart: The cart was full of fruit and cheese.
Jar: Open a jar of honey for the pancakes.
Authentic: We found an authentic Georgian restaurant.
By accident = accidently: I deleted the photo by accident.
Purchase: Keep the receipt after your purchase.
Grab: Grab your coat — the taxi is here.
See = spot: I spotted a mistake in the report.
It hit me!: Then it hit me: I left the oven on!
Stroll = walk: We strolled along the river after dinner.
Belong to: This umbrella belongs to my neighbor.
Go jogging: She goes jogging at the crack of dawn.
Yell = scream = shout: The fans screamed when the team scored.
Commit: The detective knew who committed the crime.
Put on the back burner = put off: Let's put the trip on the back burner till autumn.
Be familiar with: I'm familiar with this metro line.
Cost a pretty penny = expensive: That camera cost me a pretty penny.
It takes: It takes twenty minutes to get to the pool.
Combine: She combines work and study easily.
Put on halt: The project was put on halt for a month.
Exhausted = tired: After moving boxes all day I was exhausted.
Be honored to: I'm honored to speak at your wedding.
Aka = also known as: Ekaterina, aka Kate, will join us later.
Be scared of: As a kid I was scared of thunder.
Rewind: Rewind the video — I missed the beginning.
Tell a lie: It's easier to tell a lie than to explain.
Tell the truth: Just tell the truth — I won't be angry.
A white lie: Saying I loved the soup was a white lie.
Be left: Only one piece of cake is left.
Get together: Let's get together on Saturday evening.
Enhance: Fresh herbs enhance the taste of any soup.
Bachelor: At forty he's still a happy bachelor.
Bachelorette: The bachelorette party is on Friday.
If I'm not mistaken: If I'm not mistaken, we met last summer.
Full-time job: She left her full-time job to travel.
Part-time job: A part-time job leaves time for study.
Side hustle: His side hustle brings more than his salary.
What do you do for a living?: So, what do you do for a living? — I teach English.
Give a ride = give a drive = give a lift: My neighbor gave me a ride to the station.
Chain: It's a big chain with cafés in every district.
Empty phrase: "We'll call you back" is often an empty phrase.
The other day: The other day I ran into an old classmate.
Have a gut feeling: I have a gut feeling this plan will work.
Straight: The survey asks if you are straight, gay, or prefer not to say.
On average: On average, I read two books a month.
Recall: I can't recall where I parked.
Selfish: It's selfish to take the last piece without asking.
Put yourself first: Sometimes it's healthy to put yourself first.
Resist: I couldn't resist the fresh croissants.
Resistance: The new rules met strong resistance.
Diverse: The team is diverse: five countries, one office.
Throw smb under the bus: He threw a colleague under the bus to save himself.
Cut in = butt in = interrupt: Don't butt in — let her finish the story.
If: If it rains, we'll stay in.
Will: Maybe I will join you later — not sure yet.
Long/short-term: Learning a language is a long-term project.
We'll cross that bridge when we come to: What if the visa is denied? — We'll cross that bridge when we come to it.
Give a hand = help: Can you give me a hand with the sofa?
Put on: Put on a warm coat — it's freezing.
Take off: Take off your shoes at the door.
Numerous = plenty = a ton of: There are plenty of cafés near the office.
Different = various: The shop sells various kinds of tea.
So far so good: How's the new job? — So far so good.
Feel homesick: After a month abroad I felt homesick.
Valuable: Your feedback is really valuable to me.
Wrap/unwrap: The kids unwrapped the presents in seconds.
Wrap my head around smt = understand, re: I can't wrap my head around these tax rules.
Make a choice: You have to make a choice: the sea or the mountains.
Get on (well): We got on well from the first day.
Introduce (to): Let me introduce you to my coach.
Last for: The film lasts for three hours.
Find out: I found out about the party by accident.
Have sth/nothing/a lot in common: The twins have surprisingly little in common.
Get side-tracked: I got side-tracked and forgot to reply.
Be right up my alley: Puzzles and quizzes are right up my alley.
Gonna = going to: I'm gonna grab some coffee. Want one?
Wanna = want to: Wanna join us for a swim?
Gimme = give me: Gimme a minute, I'm almost ready.
Adore: I adore fresh bread in the morning.
Wrap: Wrap the sandwiches for the road.
Wrap up for now/today: Let's wrap up for today and continue tomorrow.
Wrap my head around sth = understand, re: It took me a week to wrap my head around the news.
Dive into = immerse into: She dived into the new book right away.
Rush: Don't rush — we have plenty of time.
Aim – target = goal: Set a clear aim before you start.
Apparent: Her talent was apparent from the first lesson.
Obvious: The answer was obvious to everyone.
Sack: They sacked him for being late every day.
Gossip: Office gossip spreads faster than email.
Rumours: Ignore the rumours — nothing is decided yet.
Doughnut: Coffee and a doughnut — my Friday tradition.
Chuckle = giggle: The kids giggled at the clumsy puppy.
Occur: The mistake occurred during the update.
It occurred to me: It occurred to me that I know her brother.
Glance = look: He glanced at his watch and rushed out.
Dairy: I avoid dairy in the evening.
Diary: She writes in her diary before bed.
Bite your tongue: I wanted to argue, but I bit my tongue.
Give smb a hand = help: Give your grandmother a hand with the bags.
No matter what: I'll finish this course no matter what.
Stick to: Let's stick to the original plan.
LLC: They registered an LLC for the family business.
Slowly but surely/steadily: Slowly but surely, my English is improving.
Screw sth up: Don't worry, everyone screws up sometimes.
Turn a blind eye to = ignore: The boss turned a blind eye to small mistakes.
Scratch the surface: One lesson only scratches the surface of grammar.
Be stuck in the rut: He felt stuck in a rut and changed his job.
Advantage/disadvantage: The main advantage of the flat is the location.
Persuade/convince: He persuaded us to stay one more day.
Be obliged to: Guests are obliged to show ID at the entrance.
Attend: I attend English lessons twice a week.
Be round the corner: Hold on, summer is round the corner.
Despite: Despite the rain, the picnic was great.
Be drained = be exhausted: After the exam I was completely drained.
Get side – tracked: We got side-tracked and talked about films for an hour.
Win-win situation: You practice English, I get help — a win-win situation.
Sense of humour: A good sense of humour saves any party.
Trait of character: Patience is my favorite trait of character in people.
Chicken feed: For him, a thousand is chicken feed.
Rather than: I'd walk rather than wait for the bus.
Anyways: Anyways, let's get back to the plan.
Seem: You seem tired today — take a nap.
On purpose = purposefully: He lost the game on purpose.
Purpose: The purpose of the meeting is to set goals.
Poached egg: A poached egg on toast is my usual breakfast.
Resist temptation: I resisted the temptation to check my phone.
Unsee: Some things you just can't unsee.
Get sick and tired of: I'm sick and tired of these endless meetings.
Go to extremes: Don't go to extremes — a small diet change is enough.
Have sth left: We have two days left before the deadline.
Backlash: The new design caused a strong backlash.
Launch: The company launches a new app in May.
Vacuum cleaner: The vacuum cleaner woke up the whole flat.
Retail: Retail prices are higher than online.
Wholesale: We buy coffee beans wholesale for the office.
I'm up for it: A picnic at dawn? I'm up for it!
Have a lot on the plate: She has a lot on her plate: work, study, kids.
I'll tell you more: The café is good. I'll tell you more — it's cheap.
Helpless: Without the internet I felt helpless.
In the middle of nowhere: The hotel was in the middle of nowhere.
In the spotlight = in the limelight: After the interview she was in the spotlight.
Poor thing: He waited two hours in the rain, poor thing.
Crutches: He was on crutches after the match.
Unbearable: The heat in the metro was unbearable.
Melting/blazing/scorching hot: It was scorching hot, so we stayed by the water.
Sunrise: We watched the sunrise from the roof.
At the break/crack of dawn: The fishermen leave at the crack of dawn.
Strict: Our coach is strict but fair.
Various = different: The menu offers various breakfast options.
Rob: Two men robbed the shop last night.
Rub: Rub the pan with garlic before cooking.
Rubber: Can I borrow your rubber? I made a mistake.
Confess: He confessed that he had eaten the last doughnut.
Draw (drew, drawn): She drew a small map of the district for me.
Love you to the moon and back = love ver: Good night, kids — love you to the moon and back.
Make/come to a conclusion: After the tests we came to a simple conclusion.
Sunbathe: They sunbathed on the roof all afternoon.
Mow the lawn: Dad mows the lawn every Saturday.
Give a ride/a drive/a lift: I'll give you a lift — it's on my way.
Prohibit: Smoking is prohibited on the platform.
In-laws: We visit my in-laws every other Sunday.
Spouse: Fill in your spouse's name in the form.
Exaggerate: Don't exaggerate — the line was only ten minutes.
Line (Am.E.) = queue (kju): There was a long queue at the bakery.
Strive: She strives to speak without mistakes.
Speaking of: Speaking of food, where shall we have lunch?
Expire: Check the date — the milk expires today.
Poor performance: The team's poor performance worried the coach.
Poor: The soup was poor, but the dessert saved the dinner.
Don't judge a book by its cover: The café looks simple, but don't judge a book by its cover.
As they say: Practice makes perfect, as they say.
Resume: After a short break, the meeting resumed.
Turn out: The "easy" route turned out to be the hardest.
Have sth in common: We found out we have a hobby in common.
We have nothing/a lot in common: We have a lot in common: books, swimming, coffee.
From my point of view = the way I see it: The way I see it, we should book now.
If you will: He's the unofficial boss here, if you will.
Expand ON it= elaborate ON it: Nice idea — could you elaborate on it?
Employee: Each employee has a personal locker.
Pervert: The tabloids called him a pervert.
Switch to: Let's switch to English for practice.
Grown up=adult: Grown-ups often forget how to play.
Judge: Don't judge people by their accent.
Graduate: She graduated from university with honors.
Drop out: He dropped out and started his own business.
Belief: It's my belief that small steps work best.
Give birth to: She gave birth to twins in May.
Cog in the machine: In that huge company he felt like a cog in the machine.
The world is your oyster: You're young and free — the world is your oyster.
Beyond: The result was beyond all expectations.
Divorce: After the divorce she moved to the coast.
Resist the temptation: Resist the temptation to answer emails at night.
Make efforts: He makes real efforts to speak only English in class.
Be overwhelmed with: I'm overwhelmed with tasks this week.
Be through the roof: Prices for flights are through the roof in July.
Get used to: You'll get used to the early mornings.
Decrease: Traffic decreases after nine in the evening.
We'll cross the bridge when we come to i: Let's not panic in advance — we'll cross the bridge when we come to it.
Envious: He was envious of their trip to Japan.
Jealous: The cat gets jealous when we pet the dog.
Ground coffee: Buy ground coffee if you don't have a grinder.
Grind: I grind the beans right before breakfast.
Would you like me to grind it for you?: You bought beans? Would you like me to grind it for you?
Could you grind it for me?: One pack of beans, please. Could you grind it for me?
Be on high alert: During exams the whole family is on high alert.
Do without: I can do without sugar, but not without coffee.
Pull one's leg = joke about sth: Relax, I'm just pulling your leg.
Asylum: The journalist asked for political asylum.
Shelter: We found shelter from the rain in a café.
Rural area: They dream of a small house in a rural area.
Permission: You need permission to park here.
First and foremost = first things first: First and foremost, thank you all for coming.
At the crack of dawn: We left for the lake at the crack of dawn.
Blazing/scorching/melting sun: We hid from the scorching sun under a tree.
Seal: The seals were sunbathing on the rocks.
Drop the bomb: At dinner she dropped the bomb: they're moving.
Hit the rock bottom: After losing the job he hit rock bottom, then slowly recovered.
Sink: The dishes are still in the sink.
Everything but the kitchen sink: She packed everything but the kitchen sink for the weekend.
Cover: Can you cover for me on Friday?
Pollution: Air pollution is lower outside the city.
At the crack/break of dawn: The bakery opens at the break of dawn.
